neighborhoods.name: Barolo
neighborhoods.address: Barolo, 12060 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The flagship Nebbiolo appellation south-west of Alba, spread across eleven communes led by La Morra, Serralunga d'Alba, Castiglione Falletto, Monforte d'Alba and Barolo village. Minimum 38 months ageing, 62 for Riserva, on calcareous-clay Tortonian and Serravallian marls.
neighborhoods.vibe: Fog-wreathed Nebbiolo amphitheatres around hilltop villages and castles; the most powerful, age-worthy red in Italy and the beating heart of the Langhe.

verified.address_quoted: Barolo, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Barbaresco
neighborhoods.address: Barbaresco, 12050 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The second great Nebbiolo DOCG north-east of Alba across Barbaresco, Neive and Treiso. Slightly shorter ageing than Barolo (26 months, 50 for Riserva) on Tortonian marls, giving aromatic, often more approachable Nebbiolo from crus like Asili, Rabaja and Martinenga.
neighborhoods.vibe: A tighter cluster of Nebbiolo crus around a medieval tower; perfumed, silkier reds that Angelo Gaja pushed onto the world stage from the 1960s.

verified.address_quoted: Barbaresco, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: La Morra
neighborhoods.address: La Morra, 12064 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The largest Barolo commune by vineyard area, on the western Tortonian marls that yield the most perfumed, earlier-approachable style of Barolo. Home to celebrated crus Brunate, Rocche dell'Annunziata and Cerequio and to the Cappella delle Brunate art chapel.
neighborhoods.vibe: Panoramic balcony over the Barolo zone with a painted chapel among the vines; the softest, most fragrant face of Nebbiolo on western marls.

verified.address_quoted: La Morra, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Serralunga d'Alba
neighborhoods.address: Serralunga d'Alba, 12050 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The eastern Barolo commune on iron-rich Serravallian soils that give the most structured, tannic and long-lived Barolo of all, with crus Vigna Rionda, Falletto, Lazzarito and Cerretta. Crowned by a 14th-century Falletti castle.
neighborhoods.vibe: A narrow ridge of austere, iron-soil Nebbiolo under a fairy-tale castle; the most powerful and slow-evolving Barolo commune.

verified.address_quoted: Serralunga d'Alba, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Castiglione Falletto
neighborhoods.address: Castiglione Falletto, 12060 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: A small central Barolo commune straddling the Tortonian-Serravallian divide, blending the perfume of La Morra with the structure of Serralunga. Holds prized crus Villero, Rocche di Castiglione, Monprivato and Bricco Boschis around a cylindrical-towered castle.
neighborhoods.vibe: A ridge-top village balanced between power and perfume; central Barolo crus that bridge the zone's two soil types.

verified.address_quoted: Castiglione Falletto, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Monforte d'Alba
neighborhoods.address: Monforte d'Alba, 12065 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The southernmost major Barolo commune, with the highest-elevation vineyards and powerful, dense Nebbiolo from the Bussia and Ginestra crus. The Bussia hamlet alone shelters some of the zone's most coveted single-vineyard sites.
neighborhoods.vibe: High, amphitheatre vineyards above a stepped medieval village famous for its summer jazz; dense, brooding Barolo from Bussia and Ginestra.

verified.address_quoted: Monforte d'Alba, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Alba
neighborhoods.address: Alba, 12051 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.classification: Langhe DOC and Barbera d'Alba DOC
neighborhoods.description: The Roman-walled market town at the centre of the Langhe and capital of the white truffle, home to the autumn Fiera Internazionale del Tartufo Bianco. Several historic houses cellar in the town, which gives its name to Barbera d'Alba, Dolcetto d'Alba and Nebbiolo d'Alba.
neighborhoods.vibe: A walled Roman town of red-brick towers and truffle markets; the commercial and gastronomic hub where the Langhe's wine houses keep their cellars.

verified.address_quoted: Alba, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Roero
neighborhoods.address: Canale, 12043 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.classification: Roero DOCG and Roero Arneis DOCG
neighborhoods.description: The sandy hills on the left bank of the Tanaro opposite the Langhe, made DOCG in 2004. Roero is Nebbiolo-based red; Roero Arneis is the aromatic white that rescued the near-extinct Arneis grape. Lighter, sandier soils than the Langhe marls.
neighborhoods.vibe: Sandy badland gorges and orchard-fringed vineyards across the Tanaro from the Langhe; home of perfumed Arneis whites and lighter Nebbiolo reds.

verified.address_quoted: Canale, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Asti and Monferrato
neighborhoods.address: Asti, 14100 Province of Asti, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.classification: Barbera d'Asti DOCG, Asti DOCG and Nizza DOCG
neighborhoods.description: The rolling Monferrato hills around Asti, a UNESCO World Heritage vineyard landscape and the engine of Piedmont's Barbera and Moscato. Barbera d'Asti DOCG and its Nizza DOCG sub-zone deliver the region's deepest Barbera; Asti and Moscato d'Asti DOCG cover the sweet sparkling and frizzante styles.
neighborhoods.vibe: UNESCO-listed Monferrato hills of Barbera and Moscato around a Palio town; the broad, generous heart of Piedmont's everyday and sparkling wines.

verified.address_quoted: Asti, Province of Asti, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Canelli
neighborhoods.address: Canelli, 14053 Province of Asti, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The historic capital of Italian sparkling wine in the southern Monferrato, where the deep tufa cellars known as the Cattedrali Sotterranee are UNESCO-listed. Canelli DOCG covers the finest dry-leaning Moscato; the town is the home of the Charmat-method Asti and Moscato traditions.
neighborhoods.vibe: A sparkling-wine town honeycombed with UNESCO-listed tufa underground cathedrals; the birthplace of Italian Moscato spumante.

verified.address_quoted: Canelli, Province of Asti, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Gavi
neighborhoods.address: Gavi, 15066 Province of Alessandria, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: Piedmont's flagship dry-white DOCG in the south-eastern hills near the Ligurian border, made from 100% Cortese. Crisp, citrus-and-mineral whites that pair with the coast's seafood; the village of Gavi gives the wine its name and a hilltop fortress.
neighborhoods.vibe: Cooler, sea-facing hills near Liguria producing Piedmont's signature dry white; crisp Cortese under a clifftop fortress.

verified.address_quoted: Gavi, Province of Alessandria, Piedmont, Italy

neighborhoods.name: Dogliani
neighborhoods.address: Dogliani, 12063 Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
neighborhoods.description: The southern Langhe town that made Dolcetto its own DOCG in 2005, the grape's most serious expression. Deep-coloured, low-acid, almond-edged reds from the hills around Dogliani, distinct from the lighter Dolcetto d'Alba and Dolcetto di Diano d'Alba.
neighborhoods.vibe: The serious home of Dolcetto in the southern Langhe; deep, plummy, almond-tinged reds built for the everyday Piedmontese table.

verified.address_quoted: Dogliani, Province of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy
